qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: A process executes the following code for(i=0; i<n; i++) fork(); The total number of child processes created is A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: A process executes the code fork(); fork(); fork(); The total number of child processes created is A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: The following C program is executed on a Unix/Linux system : #include<unistd.h> int main() { int i; for(i=0; i<10; i++) if(i%2 == 0) fork(); return 0; }

qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: Which one or more of the following options guarantee that a computer system will transition from user mode to kernel mode? A. Function Call B. malloc Call C. Page Fault D. System Call

qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: Consider the following code snippet using the fork () and wait () system calls. Assume that the code compiles and runs correctly, and that the system calls run successfully without any errors. int x=3;
